metadata.title: SHT31 Board: First Smoke and What I Learned
metadata.description: Day 8 of the SHT31 sensor board build. Applied power for the first time — and watched smoke come out of U3.

frontMatter.title: SHT31 Board: First Smoke and What I Learned

blogPosts.content:
Day 8 of the SHT31 sensor board build. Applied power for the first time — and watched smoke come out of U3.

Here's what happened, and the one change I'd make to the board spin.

{/* truncate */}

## The mistake

I populated the nRF52840 before populating Q1 — the P-MOSFET that controls OR-ing between the USB-C and coin cell power paths.

Without Q1, the coin cell positive rail was a direct wire to the LDO output. Connecting USB-C with the coin cell installed meant reverse-biasing the LDO. The TLV1117-33 has no reverse current protection. U3 died in about two seconds.

## Why it happened

The schematic review flagged the coin cell path but I focused on the signal integrity of the I2C traces and tuned out the power section. Classic mistake: the part you're least interested in is where the bug hides.

## The fix

Boards 2–5 were fine. I populated Q1 before anything else and tested the OR-ing circuit before soldering the LDO. 

Board 1 got a replacement TLV1117 — reworked with hot air, verified 3.302 V output.

## What I'd add to the silkscreen

Assembly notes. Something like:
```
POWER PATH FIRST: J1 → Q1 → U3 → VCC
Do not power until Q1 is populated.
```

It sounds obvious. It's not obvious at 11 PM.

## The actual first working reading

```
[00:00:00.438,000] I: T: 24.319000 C  RH: 52.103000 %
```

After 8 days, that log line is satisfying.
